test.name: Census extraction emits a sex assertion per persona (so minted persons get gender, not Unknown)
test.description: When extracting an 1850 census household whose Sex column states each member's sex, record-extraction must emit a `fact_type: "sex"` assertion for every persona whose sex the record states (Thomas=Male, Bridget=Female, Patrick=Male, John=Male), alongside the usual name/age/birthplace assertions. Sex is a non-fact identity attribute that `materialize_facts` reads to set the gender of any person person-evidence later mints from the record; omitting it silently creates every minted household member as `gender: "Unknown"`. This test guards the general rule (any record stating a persona's sex must yield a sex assertion), using a census as the vehicle, and keeps the existing assertion-only / pre-1880-inferred-relationship behavior as regression guards.

input.user_message:
search-records found this 1850 U.S. Census record for Thomas Flynn's household. Extract assertions for everyone in the household.

1850 United States Census — Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:MXYZ-TP4 · Dwelling 84, Family 91

| Name | Age | Sex | Occupation | Birthplace |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Thomas Flynn | 32 | M | Laborer | Ireland |
| Bridget Flynn | 7 | F |  | Ireland |
| Patrick Flynn | 5 | M |  | Ireland |
| John Flynn | 2 | M |  | Pennsylvania |

judge_context: PRIMARY CHECK: for every household member whose sex the record states (Thomas=M, Bridget=F, Patrick=M, John=M), the extractor must emit a distinct `fact_type: "sex"` assertion via extraction_append, with `value` the stated term ("Male"/"Female" or the bare "M"/"F"). Sex is a non-fact identity attribute and carries no date/place/structured_value. Emitting name/age/birthplace but NO sex assertion for the members is the failure this test exists to catch, and it is scored on Completeness.

WHY it matters (context for the judge, not a second check): materialize_facts reads a persona's sex/gender assertions to set the gender of any tree person person-evidence later mints from this record. With no sex assertion, every minted household member is silently created gender: "Unknown" even though the census states the sex plainly. The rule is GENERAL — any record that states a persona's sex (a census Sex column, a birth/death certificate sex field) must yield a sex assertion — not specific to this household.

The sex assertion is classified with the same three layers as that persona's name assertion on this record: on a pre-1940 census the informant is an unknown household_member and information_quality is typically indeterminate; evidence_type is direct where the record states the sex outright. Do not invent a different informant or mark it indirect merely because it is a census.

Unchanged behavior that must still hold (regression guards): full atomic per-person extraction (name, age/birth, birthplace) for every member; the pre-1880 relationship rule (any ParentChild inferred from household position is indirect with a `_inferred` relationship_type and the researcher as informant); and assertion-only discipline — record-extraction writes the record's S-entry and the assertions and makes ZERO tree_edit/tree_correct calls (tree_edit is not in its frontmatter). Minting the persons and setting their gender happens later in person-evidence.
Do NOT extract occupation for blank columns (Bridget/Patrick/John have no occupation listed) — absent assertions for blank fields are compliance, not incompleteness.
